mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is still near zero and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets infer that real policy rates stay deeply negative and inflation pressure persists, which lifts term-premium expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 1% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average, or if the sign correlation turns negative for two consecutive rolling quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening labor demand and slower future consumption, which lowers growth expectations and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down within the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day Treasury yield changes more than 60% of the time, or if the estimated slope on UNRATE changes becomes positive in a rolling 24-month test window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates resilient nominal demand and labor income, which strengthens growth expectations and raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive shocks in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not produce higher 20-day Treasury yields relative to single-variable baselines, or if the interaction term is insignificant and unstable across three consecutive rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening regime, rising INDPRO together with firm HOUST implies stronger real activity and rebuilding of capacity, which raises expected nominal rates and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of simultaneous growth in INDPRO and HOUST fail to predict higher 60-day Treasury yields, or if the joint effect disappears once either series is removed from the model.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS moves above 4.5%, the policy stance becomes restrictive enough that rate hikes dominate growth fears, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as duration demand rises and future easing is priced in.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 5 trading days in most instances, or if the threshold effect is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in GDPC1 following prior weakness signals stronger real demand and a firmer growth outlook, which lifts expected policy rates and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises on the latest quarterly release after a prior quarter contraction
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day Treasury yield changes, or if the average post-release yield response is indistinguishable from zero across multiple GDP upturns.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, declining UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market strength without immediate recession risk, which raises breakeven growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling unemployment and payroll gains do not jointly precede higher 20-day Treasury yields, or if the sign reverses during the same regime in more than half of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS weakens while CPIAUCSL stays elevated, real spending is deteriorating under persistent inflation, which increases recession odds and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if combined retail-sales weakness and elevated inflation do not predict lower 20-day Treasury yields, or if the interaction term is not negative and significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.
